name: pm-skills-marketplace
description: Product Management skills for AI agents

plugins.name: pm-skills
plugins.description: 68 PM skills (30 phase + 11 foundation + 12 utility + 15 tool) plus 6 sub-agents (pm-critic, pm-skill-auditor, pm-changelog-curator, pm-release-conductor, pm-workflow-orchestrator, pm-skill-router) covering the full product lifecycle. v2.30.0 is a trust-repair release: it fixes count drift across the front-door surfaces (a 2026-07-04 audit found the first line a new user reads contradicted its own sub-counts), adds a new enforcing count-phrase gate plus an advisory heading-canon gate, stages agents/ and hooks/ in the release zip while excluding docs/internal, derives the skill-builder's inventory at run time instead of hand-listing it, fixes the phase-router opt-out and a noisy hook regex, backfills four reciprocal When-NOT-to-Use edges, normalizes headings to a documented skeleton canon, rewrites eight early-cohort descriptions, freezes new dual-shell validator pairs, externalizes the trigger-eval roster, and declares the 15-skill tool family stable at 1.0.0. No new skills; catalog stays 68 / 6 sub-agents. v2.29.1 is a maintenance patch: the docs-site generator had silently dropped skill-page sections outside a fixed allow-list, hollowing about 27 of 68 pages; it now renders every section verbatim behind a new completeness gate; no new skills, catalog stays 68. v2.29.0 adds one foundation skill, foundation-build-risk-review, a pre-build risk gate returning build small, validate first, pivot first, or don't build yet, plus the pm-skill-router sub-agent that runs the new-skill collision gate and trigger router-eval key-free on the subscription; the catalog grows from 67 to 68 (foundation 10 to 11), sub-agents from 5 to 6. v2.28.0 adds one foundation skill, foundation-stakeholder-briefings, which fans any source artifact into a canonical master plus audience-tailored briefings (one per stakeholder lens, each a traceable projection of the master); the catalog grows to 67. v2.27.1 is a maintenance patch (the classification sub-count drift gate; catalog stays 66). v2.27.0 is the provable-quality release: every measured skill carries trigger-eval fixtures with CI gates for routing drift and new-skill collisions, the catalog surfaces (skill-manifest.json + the generated AGENTS.md catalog) are built from frontmatter behind enforcing staleness gates, the output-quality eval harness + asset gate ship, and the creator/validator family bakes the eval contract into skill creation; no new skills (catalog stays 66). v2.26.0 adds utility-pm-workflow-builder (guided workflow authoring to a staged review packet) and the /chain command (ad-hoc skill chains via the orchestrator Mode B); the catalog grows to 66. v2.25.2 is a maintenance patch (a unified validator-inventory manifest with an enforcing CI parity referee that closes the bash/PowerShell/CI drift class, plus the remaining 2026-06-06 Codex audit fixes); no new skills (catalog stays 65). v2.25.1 is a maintenance patch (documentation-site Pattern S reorg, a generated resource index, an em-dash-scar cleanup with new CI guards, dependency bumps, and a pre-tag validator fix); no new skills (catalog stays 65). v2.25.0 adds the plugin's first hooks: opt-in house-rule guardrails (a PreToolUse hook that blocks em-dash and en-dash when enabled via .claude/pm-skills.local.md) and a confident-only SessionStart phase router suggesting the right Triple Diamond skills, plus an advisory output-quality CI tier over the recorded samples; no new skills (catalog stays 65). v2.24.0 adds the pm-workflow-orchestrator sub-agent and its utility-pm-workflow-orchestrator dispatch skill (a governed runner that walks an ordered sequence of pm-skills against a saved foundation-prioritized-action-plan or a user-named chain, pausing for per-step go/no-go), and gives foundation-prioritized-action-plan a --run handoff (v1.1.0) that offers to run its runnable prompts through the orchestrator. v2.23.0 adds the foundation-prioritized-action-plan skill (evidence-grounded prioritized action plans via Theory of Constraints and Cynefin). v2.22.0 removes the 63 redundant per-skill command wrappers (each skill now appears once, invoked by name: /pm-skills:<name> on Claude Code, $<name> on Codex) and adds a native Codex .codex-plugin manifest. v2.21.0 is an additive distribution launch: pm-skills becomes installable through the new product-on-purpose marketplace while this self-hosted path keeps working unchanged; no new skills (catalog stays 63). v2.20.0 adds slash commands for the three workshop sprint methodologies plus documentation-count validator hardening; no new skills (catalog stays 63). v2.19.0 hardened release-validation tooling so the library polices its own counts, cross-references, and links. v2.18.0 added four highest-consensus content skills (discover-market-sizing, define-prioritization-framework, discover-journey-map, measure-survey-analysis), each with three thread-aligned samples. It carries forward v2.17.0 native Claude Code sub-agent registration: sub-agent definitions moved to the canonical agents/ directory (the coordination directory was renamed to _agent-context/ to free the name), so all 4 sub-agents auto-discover via @-mention on Claude Code; dispatch skills continue to provide the same capability on Codex CLI, Cursor, Windsurf, Copilot, and Gemini CLI. v2.17.0 also migrates skill frontmatter to the metadata-nested structure per the agentskills.io specification and makes the CI validators bash-3.2 portable. Doc-stack runs on Astro 6.3.x + Starlight 0.39.x. Carries forward v2.15.0 Sprint Skills (Foundation Sprint family of 7 + Design Sprint family of 7 + tool-note-and-vote standalone implementing Knapp/Zeratsky/Kowitz canonical sprint methodologies under classification:tool taxonomy), v2.12.0 OKR Skills set, v2.11.0 Meeting Skills Family, lean canvas, persona, 12 workflows including the foundation-to-design end-to-end arc, thread-aligned samples (Brainshelf + Storevine + Workbench), and lifecycle tools for creating, validating, and iterating skills. Follows the agentskills.io specification.
